encounter.reason: Clinical trial enrollment for first-line treatment of non-small cell lung cancer
encounter.service_provider: The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center

conditions.name: non-small cell lung cancer, stage IIIA, adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe

medications.name: folic acid 1 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: Take 1 mg by mouth once daily, starting today; must be taken for at least 7 days prior to first pemetrexed dose; continue until directed to stop

medications.name: ondansetron 8 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: Take 8 mg by mouth as pre-medication prior to chemotherapy
medications.reason: prevention of chemotherapy-induced nausea (lung cancer treatment)

medications.name: dexamethasone 4 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: Take 4 mg by mouth the day before, day of, and day after pemetrexed infusion per protocol
medications.reason: pemetrexed premedication to reduce hypersensitivity reactions and skin toxicity

medications.name: lisinopril 10 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: Take 10 mg by mouth once daily

medications.name: vitamin B12 (cyanocobalamin) 1000 mcg intramuscular injection
medications.sig: 1000 mcg intramuscular, administered in office on 2024-12-02

document_references.description: Clinical trial consent and enrollment visit. Documents informed consent discussion, trial protocol review (NCT05820295, pembrolizumab + carboplatin/pemetrexed vs carboplatin/pemetrexed for stage IIIA NSCLC), patient confirmation of understanding, consent signing, randomization to experimental arm, HIPAA research authorization for historical records from Westheimer Family Medicine and Houston Pulmonary Associates, physical examination findings, vitals, and treatment plan including Cycle 1 scheduled for January 6, 2025.

Pre-treatment medication counseling by Dr. Amy Pham, PharmD, covering chemotherapy regimen details, anticipated side effects (nausea, fatigue, myelosuppression, renal toxicity, immune-related adverse events), supportive medications initiated (folic acid, vitamin B12, ondansetron, dexamethasone), and patient education.

Signed informed consent for participation in Phase III clinical trial NCT05820295 (pembrolizumab + carboplatin/pemetrexed vs carboplatin/pemetrexed alone for unresectable stage III NSCLC). Includes HIPAA research authorization for access to historical medical records from Westheimer Family Medicine and Houston Pulmonary Associates covering January 1, 2022 through present. Witnessed by Jennifer Liu, RN.
